event.summary: Claimed one ready iteration unit without expanding repository or path scope.

acceptance.proof: The composer accepts only existing accepted T, U, and V acquisition types; it performs no provider call or ambient acquisition.
Implementation, runtime, and transport witnesses move into three separately inspectable owner artifacts without comparison, merging, cloning, or inferred common epoch/revision/currentness.
All eleven original value allocations move in fixed T/U/V lane and O role order through one complete LSLC-001S admission while witness allocations remain separately retained.
Caller-selected S limits are delegated unchanged and any S admission rejection remains typed; the composer adds no alternate field acceptance or complete-snapshot policy.

No cross-owner atomicity, common witness, freshness, currentness, authorization, activation, ambient clock/host/interface/network acquisition, endpoint semantics, sockets, runtime effects, devices, dependencies/features, or Manifold authority is added.

document.commit_policy: Commit the T/U/V-to-S consuming composition separately from acquisition providers, ambient platform adapters, runtime activation, protocol/transport behavior, devices, and authority.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route the three-owner evidence-preserving composition and prohibit cross-owner atomicity/currentness inference.
Describe complete S admission from accepted acquisitions without promoting it to runtime activation or authority.
Review multi-owner evidence composition guidance; retain unchanged because artifact and authority separation already require distinct owner receipts.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review installed skill without public mutation.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Review Rusty LSL/Manifold routing; retain unchanged because no repo ownership or runtime route changes.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review installed skill without public mutation.

non_scope: Provider selection or calls, retries, background work, clock/environment/hostname/interface inspection, identity generation, address/port parsing, sockets, network reads/writes, reachability, authorization, or platform adapters.
Cross-owner identity equality, common epoch/revision, atomic acquisition, freshness/currentness beyond each already accepted witness, witness aggregation, or a new authority decision.

Modification of O/S acceptance policy, XML/document behavior, protocol, wire, discovery, inlet, outlet, buffering, recovery, FFI, runtime activation, devices, packages, permissions, dependencies, features, or effects.
Manifold admission, registry, subscription, route, lease, revision, replay, authorization, audit, session, topology, transport, command, product, or provider authority.
document.objective: Consume one accepted T implementation acquisition, one accepted U runtime acquisition, and one accepted V transport acquisition; preserve their witnesses as three separately inspectable owner artifacts; move all eleven original opaque value allocations into one complete LSLC-001S snapshot admission; and add no cross-owner atomicity/currentness inference, ambient acquisition, runtime effect, device, dependency/feature, or Manifold authority.
outputs: Allocation-preserving consuming part access for accepted T, U, and V acquisitions and their grouped value types where required.
A three-owner evidence object retaining implementation, runtime, and transport witnesses separately without comparison or aggregation.
A complete accepted LSLC-001S snapshot paired with the separate three-owner evidence object.
Focused positive, pointer-preservation, delegated-limit rejection, no-cross-owner-inference, and ambient-surface validation.
